title: Option Full Open Interest
description: "Open-interest updates for every option contract in one subscription."
---

<!-- @generated by `generate_docs_site` from fpss_event_schema.toml. Do not edit by hand. -->

# Option Full Open Interest

Streams the morning open-interest reports for every option contract — one subscription covering the entire OPRA universe. Each report delivers an `OpenInterest` event.

The snippets below assume a connected client with streaming started — see [Getting Started](/streaming/) for the connect-and-stream ladder.

<SdkTabs>

<template #rust>

```rust
use thetadatadx::fpss::protocol::SecTypeExt;
use thetadatadx::fpss::{FpssData, FpssEvent};
use thetadatadx::SecType;

tdx.start_streaming(|event: &FpssEvent| {
    if let FpssEvent::Data(FpssData::OpenInterest { contract, open_interest, .. }) = event {
        println!("{} oi={open_interest}", contract.symbol);
    }
})?;

let sub = SecType::Option.full_open_interest();
tdx.subscribe(sub.clone())?;

// Remove this stream; the session stays open for other subscriptions.
tdx.unsubscribe(sub)?;
```

</template>

<template #python>

```python
from thetadatadx import SecType

def on_event(event):
    if event.kind == "open_interest":
        print(event.contract.symbol, event.open_interest)

tdx.start_streaming(on_event)

sub = SecType.OPTION.full_open_interest()
tdx.subscribe(sub)

# Remove this stream; the session stays open for other subscriptions.
tdx.unsubscribe(sub)
```

</template>

<template #typescript>

```typescript
import { SecType } from 'thetadatadx';

tdx.startStreaming((event) => {
  if (event.kind === 'open_interest') {
    const e = event.openInterest!;
    console.log(e.contract.symbol, e.openInterest);
  }
});

const sub = SecType.option().fullOpenInterest();
tdx.subscribe(sub);

// Remove this stream; the session stays open for other subscriptions.
tdx.unsubscribe(sub);
```

</template>

<template #cpp>

```cpp
client.set_callback([](const tdx::FpssEvent& event) {
    if (event.kind == TDX_FPSS_OPEN_INTEREST) {
        auto& e = event.open_interest;
        std::cout << e.contract.symbol << " oi=" << e.open_interest << "\n";
    }
});

auto sub = tdx::SecType::option().full_open_interest();
client.subscribe(sub);

// Remove this stream; the session stays open for other subscriptions.
client.unsubscribe(sub);
```

</template>

<template #http>

```http
GET ws://127.0.0.1:25520/v1/events
```

WebSocket streaming from the bundled [server binary](/server/). Send one JSON envelope per command; set `"add": false` to unsubscribe.

**Example**

```bash
websocat ws://127.0.0.1:25520/v1/events
{"msg_type": "STREAM", "sec_type": "OPTION", "req_type": "FULL_OPEN_INTEREST", "id": 1, "add": true}
```

</template>

</SdkTabs>

## Event fields

Each update arrives as a `OpenInterest` event with these fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `contract` | contract | Resolved contract identity (symbol, security type, and option fields). |
| `ms_of_day` | i32 | Milliseconds since midnight Eastern Time. |
| `open_interest` | i32 | Total outstanding contracts. |
| `date` | i32 | Trading date as a YYYYMMDD integer. |
| `received_at_ns` | u64 | Local receive timestamp, nanoseconds since the Unix epoch. |

The `contract` field carries `symbol`, the security type, and — for options — `expiration`, `right`, and the strike. See [Handling Events](/streaming/events) for the full event catalogue and per-language field shapes.
